subject: process_model
what: no persistent CLI processes; each turn is a fresh shell-out
why: 2026-04-05 spike showed all three CLIs expose non-interactive JSONL mode with session resumption; pty/tmux was dead code

subject: concurrency
what: one in-flight turn per conversation, FIFO queue
why: CLIs are single-threaded interactive processes; principle #1 seam requires serialization above the adapter

subject: filesystem
what: each CLI turn runs in an ephemeral scratch cwd; no access to user files
why: OpenAI chat API has no cwd concept; only coherent interpretation is to suppress CLI native file tools at the sandbox

subject: claude_exit_code
text: claude -p exits 0 even when a rate_limit_event with status != allowed was emitted. Never infer health from exit code; always inspect the JSONL event stream for rate_limit_event and result.subtype.

subject: cold_cache_tax
text: Cold CLI invocation eats 6k (gemini) to 14k (codex) input tokens of agent-prompt overhead before the user's prompt is even processed. claude's prompt-cache window is 1h. If jRouter idles >1h, first turn back pays the full tax. Warm-cache discipline is a routing concern, not just an optimization.

subject: agent_contamination
text: Under OAuth subscription auth, the backend CLIs run as tool-wielding agents framed by their own system prompts. A trivial prompt ('reply ok') produced num_turns:3 in the spike — internal agent work the client didn't request. This is observable (log num_turns and tool events per turn) but not preventable. Design for it, don't fight it.

subject: gemini_compound
text: Gemini auto-routes across sub-models within a single turn (spike saw gemini-3-flash-preview and gemini-2.5-flash-lite in the same call). stats.models has the per-model breakdown. GeminiAdapter must surface this as a compound-provider quota event, not a single counter.

subject: session_id_shapes
text: Three vendors, three session id shapes: claude accepts a client-chosen UUID via --session-id; gemini returns a server-assigned index in its init event; codex returns a server-assigned thread_id in thread.started. The adapter boundary must normalize this — the router stores an opaque string and never parses it.

goal: scaffold the orientation loop (STATUS, JOURNAL, CLAUDE.md, scripts/)

text: canonical project name is FreelOAder (package: freeloader); old 'jRouter'/'jrouter' references in non-journal files have been renamed
subject: project_name
why: repo dir, PLAN.md, README.md all use freeloader; the jrouter references in STATUS/ROADMAP/AGENT/CLAUDE/scripts were drift from a prior name and would break every gate on the first commit that creates src/

text: CLIAdapter.send() Protocol carries system: str | None = None from day one
subject: system_prompt_slot
why: phase-5 tool-call shim requires adapter-owned system-prompt slot (PLAN.md § 'Things to explicitly *not* do'); retrofitting the signature after ClaudeAdapter/GeminiAdapter/CodexAdapter exist is a three-way rewrite plus three contract-test rebaselines

text: chat-completions history diff lives in src/freeloader/canonical/history_diff.py — not the frontend, not the adapter
subject: history_diff_home
why: principle #6 says handlers are ~50 lines; decision #3 says adapters are text-in/text-out. Client edits/regenerations/reordered system messages need a real home. Canonical layer is the only coherent owner
source: PLAN.md principle #4, decision recorded in phase-1 exit criteria
